name: APC 2023 Fallback Summaries

source.notes: Structured APC-derived summaries for AI fallback intake generation. These are not rule-complete pathway JSONs and should only be used when no strong approved JSON pathway exists.

usageRules: Use these summaries only in AI fallback mode when pathway classification confidence is low or no suitable approved JSON pathway exists.
Do not generate diagnoses or treatment plans from these summaries alone.
Deterministic triage and clinician review remain the source of urgency and final clinical decisions.

summaries.title: Chest Pain
urgentSubjectiveRedFlags: Is the pain severe right now?
Did the pain start at rest or with very little effort?
Do you also have shortness of breath?
Does the pain spread to your arm, jaw, neck, or back?
Are you sweaty, pale, faint, or nauseous with the pain?
clinicianObjectiveFocus: BP, pulse, oxygen saturation, respiratory rate
Urgent ECG if indicated
Assess for ACS and cardiorespiratory instability

aiFallbackGuidance.ordering: Ask urgent cardiorespiratory red flags first, then timing, radiation, exertional features, and associated symptoms.
aiFallbackGuidance.notes: Do not let AI reassure. If severe/urgent features are present, return structured urgent-intake questions only.

summaries.title: Abdominal Pain / Heartburn
urgentSubjectiveRedFlags: Is the pain severe or getting rapidly worse?
Are you pregnant or could you be pregnant?
Have you had vomiting that will not stop?
Have you had no stool or wind for the past day?
Are your eyes or skin yellow?
Have you fainted or felt very weak with the pain?
coreSubjectiveQuestions: Any vomiting, fever, diarrhoea, constipation, urinary symptoms, or vaginal bleeding?
Any recent pregnancy, miscarriage, or delivery?
Any relation to meals or heartburn symptoms?
clinicianObjectiveFocus: BP and general stability
Abdominal guarding, rebound, rigidity, mass
Consider pelvic/urinary/pregnancy-related causes depending on history

aiFallbackGuidance.ordering: Ask immediate danger and pregnancy-related questions first, then location, onset, vomiting, bowel, urinary, and gynecological features.
aiFallbackGuidance.notes: Use as fallback only; avoid overextending into clinician exam questions.

summaries.title: Injured Patient
urgentSubjectiveRedFlags: Is there heavy bleeding right now?
Did you lose consciousness?
Is the injury to the head, neck, chest, or abdomen?
Can you move the injured limb normally?
Is there severe deformity, exposed bone, or numbness/coldness below the injury?
coreSubjectiveQuestions: Any weakness, numbness, or severe pain?
Any chest pain or difficulty breathing after injury?
clinicianObjectiveFocus: BP and signs of shock
Perfusion below injury
Open fracture, severe deformity, head injury red flags

aiFallbackGuidance.ordering: Ask immediate life/limb danger questions first, then mechanism, region, mobility, and head injury symptoms.
aiFallbackGuidance.notes: AI should stay in subjective history mode and not invent trauma management.

summaries.title: Headache
urgentSubjectiveRedFlags: Did the headache start suddenly and severely?
Do you also have vomiting, confusion, weakness, or fits?
Do you have fever with the headache?
Have you recently had head injury?
Are you pregnant with severe headache?
coreSubjectiveQuestions: Any visual changes, weakness, numbness, or speech problems?
Have you had headaches like this before?
clinicianObjectiveFocus: Neurological red flags
BP, temperature, glucose if indicated
Assess for meningitis, stroke/TIA, head injury, severe hypertension

aiFallbackGuidance.ordering: Ask catastrophic and neuro red flags first, then onset, associated symptoms, pregnancy/head injury context, and recurrence.
aiFallbackGuidance.notes: Fallback questions should be biased toward ruling in urgent review.

summaries.title: Fever
urgentSubjectiveRedFlags: Are you confused, very weak, or struggling to breathe?
Do you have severe headache, rash, or vomiting blood?
Have you recently travelled to a malaria area?
Do you have cough, night sweats, or weight loss?
Are you pregnant?
coreSubjectiveQuestions: How long have you had the fever?
Any cough, sore throat, urinary symptoms, diarrhoea, abdominal pain, rash, or body pain?
Any TB symptoms such as weight loss or night sweats?
Any HIV or chronic illness context if known?
clinicianObjectiveFocus: Temperature, pulse, BP, respiratory rate, oxygen saturation
Screen for COVID/TB and dangerous sepsis-like features

aiFallbackGuidance.ordering: Ask instability, breathing, rash, bleeding, pregnancy, and malaria/TB screening questions first, then organ-system symptom review.
aiFallbackGuidance.notes: Use this only if no stronger fever-oriented JSON pathway is available.

summaries.title: Collapse / Blackout / Faint
urgentSubjectiveRedFlags: Did you completely lose consciousness?
Do you have chest pain, palpitations, or shortness of breath?
Did you have a seizure or shaking movements?
Did you hit your head or injure yourself?
Are you confused or weak afterwards?
coreSubjectiveQuestions: How long were you out for?
Any warning symptoms before it happened?
Any diabetes, pregnancy, or dehydration context?
clinicianObjectiveFocus: BP, pulse, glucose, neurological status
Assess for cardiac, neurological, metabolic, or dehydration causes

aiFallbackGuidance.ordering: Ask true loss-of-consciousness and cardiopulmonary red flags first, then prodrome, duration, injury, and recurrence.
aiFallbackGuidance.notes: Good fallback for unclear faint/collapse complaints.

summaries.title: Dizziness
urgentSubjectiveRedFlags: Did you faint or almost faint?
Do you have chest pain, palpitations, or shortness of breath?
Do you have weakness, numbness, speech trouble, or severe headache?
Do you have vomiting and cannot keep fluids down?
coreSubjectiveQuestions: Is it spinning, lightheadedness, or imbalance?
Any ear symptoms, headache, fever, visual changes, or dehydration?
clinicianObjectiveFocus: BP, pulse, glucose if needed
Consider neurological, cardiac, dehydration, ENT causes

aiFallbackGuidance.ordering: Screen for collapse/stroke/cardiac danger first, then characterize the dizziness and associated symptoms.
aiFallbackGuidance.notes: Fallback intake should distinguish spinning vs faintness if possible.

summaries.title: Nausea / Vomiting
urgentSubjectiveRedFlags: Are you unable to keep down any fluids?
Are you vomiting blood?
Do you also have severe abdominal pain, headache, or confusion?
Are you pregnant or could you be pregnant?
Have you had diarrhoea, jaundice, or no stool/wind?
coreSubjectiveQuestions: Any abdominal pain, diarrhoea, fever, headache, dizziness, or urinary symptoms?
Any recent pregnancy, alcohol/drug exposure, or chronic illness?
clinicianObjectiveFocus: Hydration and shock features
BP, pulse, glucose if clinically indicated
Link to abdominal pain, pregnancy, or diarrhoea causes

aiFallbackGuidance.ordering: Ask dehydration, bleeding, severe pain, pregnancy, and neuro red flags first, then pattern and associated GI symptoms.
aiFallbackGuidance.notes: Keep the AI in symptom-clarification mode only.

summaries.title: Urinary Symptoms
urgentSubjectiveRedFlags: Are you unable to pass urine?
Do you have fever, vomiting, or flank/back pain?
Are you pregnant?
Is there visible blood in the urine?
coreSubjectiveQuestions: Is it pain, frequency, urgency, poor stream, or retention?
Any lower abdominal pain, vaginal symptoms, or back pain?
clinicianObjectiveFocus: Temperature, BP, hydration/stability
Differentiate lower UTI vs more severe urinary involvement

aiFallbackGuidance.ordering: Ask retention, fever/flank pain, pregnancy, and blood-in-urine questions first, then classic LUTS characterization.
aiFallbackGuidance.notes: Useful when abdominal/pelvic complaints are vague.
